Sentence: I felt ... when I was caught under pricing a water melon in a local supermarket. I did it because I was feeling not too well at the time. Never went back to do shopping there.
I felt ... when I did not volunteer to drive my parents back from London, when they said mum did not want dad to drive in the dark.
I felt ... when I shouted at my children with a lost temper which was uncalled for. Of course I apologised, but I carried the ... of it.
I felt ... when I saw a man hit a woman in the street and did not step in to stop it.

I felt ... when a boy had his foot ran over by the school bus when we were crowding round to be first aboard. We all pushed him so his foot went under the wheel. To cap it all, I saw the bus door open in front of me and jumped in, when I should have backed off to get out of the way.
I felt ... when I didn’t do as well in my exams as I should have. I was so ... I hid the results from everyone and told them I got a good mark.
I felt ... when I was caught out lying to a friend. I told her that I had flu and could not go out. However she then found out that I had been out with another friend.
I felt ... when I didn’t take my cat to the vets for a while and she got ill. I felt ... and thought it was my fault, even though it wasn’t. I couldn’t focus on anything because I felt so guilty.
I felt ... when I stole money from my mum.
I felt ... when I forgot to set an alarm and was 2 hours late to go and let my mum's dog out for a walk and feed him.
I feel ... because I owe more money than I should and don't tell.
I felt ... when I smacked my granddaughter in public when she was being very naughty.
I felt ... when I offended an old man in the supermarket.

I felt ... when I had to leave my dog, whilst I went on holiday. I already felt ..., but when I said goodbye to her, she looked so sad which made me feel even worse. The ... continued whilst I was away when I heard she was pining for me at the door.
I felt ... because I had been told not to put my used monthly protection in a certain bin and I did, and then I lied about doing it.
I felt ... when I forgot a relative's birthday.
I felt ... when I ate my whole Easter egg in one go.
I felt ... when I lied about the reliability of a second hand bicycle I sold, neglecting to mention a problem with the crankset.
I felt ... when I forgot our anniversary.
I felt ... when I shoplifted as a child because my sister talked me into it and I should have known better.

I felt … when, as a teenager, I found a lip gloss on the ground outside. I didn't want to use it as I didn't know where it had been, so I put it in a friend's desk with a note which I disguised my handwriting in, saying it was a present. Thankfully she didn't drop dead from a contagious disease, but I still feel a bit ...!

I felt ... because I had to make the decision to have my chocolate labrador put down, she was struggling to walk. It was a such a hard decision because I wanted to keep her with me, she was outstanding, so good and perceptive. I know she couldn't have carried on as as she was and the vet said she had lived longer than a lot of labradors, but I still felt/feel ... for having to make that decision.
I felt ... because I walked past a beggar and didn't bother to stop and give her a pound.
I felt ... because my grandfather passed away and I was not there because I didn't want to drive in the early hours of the morning.
I felt ... because I did something silly.
I felt ... because I cheated on my husband.
I felt ... when I had an extra marital affair. It was not until I was found out that I realised the hurt and pain I had caused.
I felt ... because I wasn't there when my mother died.
I felt ... that I lied about consuming the last of the milk this morning, letting my son take the blame from his mother.
I felt ... when I realized how selfish I was being to someone recently. The situation is none of my business, so I'll butt out.
I felt ... when I said "no" to helping someone out simply because I couldn't be bothered.
I felt ... after telling my aunt a little white lie as I didn't want to take her to a hospital appointment. I'm ... of my laziness.
I felt ... when I shouted at someone for no good reason
I felt ... when I shouted at a colleague for not doing the job properly and had to apologise.
I felt ... when I realized I had pulled out a pair of pants from my bag when I was getting my wallet out
I felt ... about having let my debt situation get so bad that I ended up with a CCJ.
I felt ... when I had to file my divorce petition because I had to reveal the causes why my marriage failed.
I felt ... when I found money and did not hand it in.
I felt ... when I scraped another car and drove off, so I drove back there to leave my Insurance details.
I felt ... when my step moms’ brother said something funny just after she’d died, and I laughed. Everyone in the room was staring at me and I felt like a complete idiot for laughing.
I felt ... because I was caught out lying about my diary engagements to escape a celebration meal with my inlaws.
I felt ... because I forgot to do something that I promised I would do for someone.
I felt ... when I left the house and forgot to lock up, not realising my mistake for several hours.
I felt ... when I said something inappropriate to a friend and then wished I had never opened my mouth.
I felt ... when I told my boyfriend his favourite programme wasn’t on the telly because he watches it all the time and I wanted to watch something else.
I felt ... when I weighed myself after Christmas.
I felt ... when I argued with old woman and called her stupid.
I felt ... because I was a quizmaster at a party and I made a mess of it as I hadn't prepared adequately.
I felt ... when I drank too much and fell asleep.

I felt ... when I encouraged my mother to report a dangerous driver, then she got attacked and nearly died by the driver when the police told the person she reported that it was her that did it. I felt ... because it was my fault she even went to the police about it in the first place and I nearly caused her to die.
I felt ... when I recall that I cheated on my husband, something I should never have done and now wish I hadn’t.
I felt … when I crashed my grandfather's car.
I felt ... when I was in a hurry and passed by a charity worker asking for donations.
I felt ... when I pretended to be less able bodied than I am so I could get a seat on a busy train.
I feel … because I have forgotten to renew my library books and those of my elderly neighbour and was worried that she would get a fine.
I felt ... because I forgot someone’s name when they came to visit.
I feel ... because I didn't spend as much time as I should with my ill brother before he died.
I felt ... because other people knew that I hadn't been seeing my ill brother as often as I could have been doing.
I felt ... because I hadn't called my friend who lost a close friend because I was so busy with my own life that I neglected to think of her.

I felt ... because my Grandmother, 8 years ago, was in a nursing home and was only given a short time to live as she was very ill. On the day she died, my Mum phoned me to tell me she had gotten worse and to come see her but, I took my time thinking a few hours wouldn't make a difference but, it did and I arrived less than 20 minutes after she passed away. I have felt ... about that ever since as I should have been there, not only to say goodbye but also for my Mum to support her.
I felt ... because at school, I took my friend's book from his backpacks. I was afraid to admit it.
I felt ... when I called in sick for work one day when I wasn’t really sick.
I felt ... when I made a mistake at work and somebody else was blamed.
I felt ... when I went on holiday and left our cat behind.
I felt ... when I failed my ninth year at high school.
I felt ... when I over-ate on snacks.
I felt ... that I lied about my employment experience on a job application letter.
I felt ... because I haven't been as successful in my life as friends have been.
I felt ... because I copied large sections of an English essay on Gothic literature and neglected to give credit where it was due.
I felt ... because I was supposed to buy an urgent item for my partner but I forgot and the shops were closed by the time I remembered.
I felt ... when I took a sickie from work because my dog was ill.
I felt ... when we saw my partner's sister on Boxing Day and had not realized she would be at his parents' place and had bought us gifts. We did not have anything to give her.
I felt ... when I lost my temper with my son.
I feel ... because I didn't feel sorry for somebody going through a hard time.

I felt ... when a supposed friend of mine punched me in the back while I was bent down to tie a shoelace and, without thinking, I spun and punched him in the chest and, luckily, didn't break his fingers though they swelled up. I felt ... that I had reacted like that but it was an instinct to being punched in such a cowardly way.
I felt ... for denying to offer my kids what they demanded of me.

I felt ... when I was about seventeen and went on a long car trip with my boyfriend, he ran out of petrol and neither of us had any money so I had to beg money in a services to try to get enough petrol to get us home.
I felt ... when I was stopped for driving over the limit.
I felt ... when I ate  my daughter's burger from McDonald and she was upset after that.
I felt ... when I cheated in an exam and I was caught.
I felt ... when I realised I was boasting about all the holidays I had to look forward to and remembered my best friend wouldn't have any as she couldn't afford it.
I felt ... that I had promised to make a charitable donation this Christmas instead of buying a particular present for somebody, and never actually got around to it.
I felt ... when I forgot to take my Christmas present for my sister, I left it at home, and she was generously hosting Christmas Day at her house.
I felt ... when my mom caught me lying.
I felt ... when I had too much to drink in a pub, fell over and had to go to hospital.
I felt ... when I remember being part of a group of children at school who verbally bullied another child.
I felt ... because I had put on weight and my work trousers didn't fit.
I felt ... when I started eating a box of Christmas fudge, and ate the whole thing, after a pizza.

I felt ... one night when I was younger, I went out for a night drinking with a friend and I ended up dancing and kissing a girl in a club, despite the fact I already had a girlfriend, and I felt so much ... the next couple of days that I broke up with my girlfriend the next time we spoke.
I felt ... when I gave a postcard to a friend with a photo of some deer with big ears on it, as he had ears that stuck out, and he never said a word to me about it.
I felt ... when I realised that I had forgotten my best friend's birthday when she has had such a tough year but never forgets my birthday or special events.

I felt ... that when I was in my teen years I never did well at school. On one occasion I brought home a school report card which was full of the just about the worst grades you can get.  My mother showed her displeasure towards me.
I felt ... when I was younger and I was caught cheating on a test in school. The teacher had seen me copying off someone else and gave me a warning about this.
I felt shame when I let down a friend who was relying on me to do a job at his house.
I feel ... because I am still in bed at 12:30 pm.  Wish I had got up earlier.
I felt ... when I had a car accident and put two teenagers boys in hospital.

I felt ... at Halloween time many years ago, my friends and I were out playing and decided to play pranks on our neighbours. One elderly lady that lived near us was the first house we called to. The lady got very frightened when she saw a group of young lads at her door and I knew that this was not right. I told all of my friends that we shouldn't be doing this to old ladies, some agreed, some didn't, but I felt ... all the same.

I feel ... because I behave in a way that I am not proud of. I feel ... when I walk past homeless people in the street without offering any help or money. The ... is because I feel that I am responsible for doing something but I do not know how to help. I have been told I should not give them money but it is so hard when it is cold.
I felt ... giving a cheque to the managing agent which did not cover all I owed.
I felt ... when I was an air stewardess on a flight and I announced to the passengers that we were arriving in Edinburgh, when really it was London Heathrow.
